strategy_criteria_summary: USA optionable stocks over $20 with market cap above $2B, 3M average volume above 1M, beta above 1, price above the 50/200-day SMAs, within 20% of the 52-week high, and RSI 40-70. This keeps the structure-driven pool liquid and tradeable without over-filtering it to zero.
scoring_reasons: trend alignment was clearly supportive|relative strength was clearly supportive|news/catalyst quality was mixed rather than decisive|best contract was long_call 2026-05-08 71|News flow was mixed rather than one-way.|Sector trend does not fully support the thesis.
decision_reasoning: MP shows the best blend of direction quality and contract economics. Trend is strongly supportive (+28% 20d, +18% 50d) with relative strength of +21% vs SPY and +29% vs sector, backed by a fresh Wedbush Outperform at $90. The 5/8 long 71 call requires only a ~5% move to breakeven, has the best liquidity in the chain, and 12% spread is acceptable. IONQ has stronger momentum but 114% IV creates severe post-earnings crush risk on a long call. SPG is a long put setup with weak liquidity (OI thin, 29% spread on the 190) and weak thesis. CSCO requires a ~9% move to breakeven on low-delta OTM calls. Score lands in the Standard recommend band given mixed news flow and soft sector context.
